reason: Original evidence (recorded inline in this story's evidence/index.md — no separate per-record file exists) pinned base commit SHA 733ef3e, which is not an ancestor of current HEAD (side effect of the e8cda6b squash noted by the wave-07 autopsy, finding H-8, remediation R-6). Per impl/governance/evidence-policy.md's revision-pinning rule, this addendum re-runs the cited command against the current, ancestor-valid HEAD and records the result as status `retested`, without overwriting the original index.md row.
execution_command: go test -v on the seven named CS-16 packages (kernel/database, kernel/jobs, kernel/outbox, kernel/workflow, kernel/auth, kernel/mfa, kernel/httpclient) with the exact seven-benchmark regex, -benchmem -count=1. Original cited -benchtime=10x; capped to -benchtime=2x per this remediation's bench-budget cap (smaller of the citation and 2x).
result: PASS: all seven benchmarks (BenchmarkTenantTransactionOpenCommit, BenchmarkJobClaimFinalize, BenchmarkRelayDispatchBatch, BenchmarkDefinitionValidate, BenchmarkTokenVerify, BenchmarkTOTPDerive, BenchmarkGuardedDial) executed and reported ns/op, B/op, allocs/op (unchanged coverage; absolute timings vary by run/host as expected for wall-clock benchmarks).
